type="application/ld+json">[{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Lucky Labrador ","url":"https://www.meetup.com/silent-books-club/events/313501787/","description":"Come anytime, bring any book book you're reading :).

No pressure to socialize, you can just dive into your book if you want.

No pressure to read, come and hang out!
Can't wait to meet you!","startDate":"2026-03-10T02:00:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-3-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Lucky Labrador","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"915 SE Hawthorne, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"PDX Silent Book Club","url":"https://www.meetup.com/silent-books-club/"},"performer":"PDX Silent Book Club"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Psychedelics and Buddhism ","url":"https://www.meetup.com/psychedelic/events/313153756/","description":"**Our Regular Sessions Include**

* Meditation: We open and close each meeting with a meditation session, creating a mindful atmosphere to engage and reflect.
* Engaging Discussions: Delve into fascinating topics such as the history of psychedelics in Buddhist practices, the concept of ego dissolution, and the parallels between meditative states and psychedelic experiences.
* Deep Dive into the Literature: Explore thought-provoking chapters and imagery from books (like Zig Zag Zen: Buddhism and Psychedelics) and scientific research.
* Sutra Exploration: Compare the profound experiences described in ancient sutras, like The Heart Sutra, with those reported in psychedelic journeys.

**Why You Should Join**

Expand Your Understanding: Gain insights that might inform your psychedelic experiences and possibly your Buddhist practices.

Community and Connection: Share experiences and perspectives with diverse individuals who share your interest in these profound subjects.

Mindful Exploration: Approach the topic with a balance of curiosity and respect, grounded in meditation and mindful discussion.

**Everyone Is Welcome!**

Whether you’re a Zen practitioner, follow another Buddhist tradition, or have no religious affiliation but carry a deep interest in these topics, our meetup is a space for respectful exploration and shared learning.

🌌 Embrace the journey of mind-expanding discussions and spiritual growth. We can't wait to explore the synergies of Psychedelics and Buddhism with you! 🌌

RSVP Today and be part of a unique exploration that bridges ancient wisdom and contemporary psychedelic insights. See you there! 🙏🌺🍄

**About the Moderator**

Zack Prager works on building user experiences that make people happier, healthier, and more mindful at [Mindful Labs](https://www.mindfullabs.com/technology). He studied software development at UCLA and Positive Psychology (science of happiness) at the University of Pennsylvania under Dr. Martin Seligman.

He has a long-standing meditation and yoga practice and writes and produces music for breath work, yoga, and meditation ([Mango Dass](https://mangodass.com/) on Spotify).

His work has been covered by the SF Chronicle, SD Tribune, ABC, CW, NBC, Brit+Co, & more, as well as praised by leading academics.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**Portland Psychedelic Society** (PPS) is a donation-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.

We ask for a [sliding scale donation of $0-$10](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/donate#DonationFormEmbed) to attend this event. You are not required to pay and no one will be turned away. Cash, Card, PayPal and Venmo are accepted.

PPS Members: your seat is always free!

**[Visit our website](http://www.portlandpsychedelic.org)** for info about [PPS memberships](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/become-a-member), [volunteer opportunities](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/volunteer), and much more.

Thanks for your participation and support!","startDate":"2026-03-10T01:30:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-5-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Taborspace","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"5441 SE Belmont St, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Portland Psychedelic Society","url":"https://www.meetup.com/psychedelic/"},"performer":"Portland Psychedelic Society"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Bouldering & Social Beginner-Friendly Climbing - Come try the new expanded gym!","url":"https://www.meetup.com/adventure-society/events/313624097/","description":"Come join us for a bouldering session at the Circuit SW bouldering gym! We’ll grab food and bevvies afterwards if the group is down.

Climbers of all abilities are welcome! We generally have a good mix of first-timers up to V4/V5 climbers.

Day pass is $20 and shoe rental is $4. Chalk is also available for rent, but I will have a bag to share.

If it’s your first time, keep in mind you’ll have to fill out a waiver and watch a safety video. So plan an extra 10 mins or so to get that done before the event starts.

Meet over by the wall next to the water fountain. I always have glasses and a bright yellow chalk bag. Before the event starts I also generally make a post of what I’m wearing so I’m easier to spot.","startDate":"2026-03-10T01:35:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-4-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Circuit","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"6050 Sw Macadam, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Adventure Society 20s to 40s","url":"https://www.meetup.com/adventure-society/"},"performer":"Adventure Society 20s to 40s"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Decompress + Connect Meditation Group","url":"https://www.meetup.com/decompress-connect/events/313408132/","description":"Decompress and connect at the end of your work week with meditation practice and community. This event is in partnership with the Young Meditators Group of Portland.

This open session offers 20 minutes of silent meditation followed by a meditative talking circle practice, and the opportunity to connect and socialize afterwards at a local pub.

**No experience required, and meditators of all traditions are welcome.**
**Cushions will be provided but feel free to bring your own.**

**SCHEDULE**

**6:30 – 7:00PM Silent Meditation**
Silent meditation practice (you are welcome to practice a silent meditation of your choosing)

**7:00 – 7:25PM Meditative Talking Circle**
Resting on the foundation of meditation practice, this talking circle practice expands to include others in the circle. While maintaining a meditation practice, members of the circle are welcome to speak from the present moment, though speaking is not required to participate. Instructions will be provided at the beginning so please arrive on time for this part of the practice.

**7:25PM Closing and Social**
There will be some time after group to connect after practice, after which the group may choose to go out for drinks and snacks at a nearby pub within walking distance.

This is a 'by donation' event. Suggested donation amount $3-10: [Please Donate Here](https://www.paypal.com/donate?hosted_button_id=PPCNJX8KYKY42)

If you are a regular member, please consider signing up for a monthly donation- it helps make this group sustainable!","startDate":"2026-03-10T01:30:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-5-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"TaborSpace","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"5441 Belmont room 04, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Decompress + Connect Meditation Group","url":"https://www.meetup.com/decompress-connect/"},"performer":"Decompress + Connect Meditation Group"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"PDX Code & Coffee @ DeadStock Beaverton","url":"https://www.meetup.com/portland-code-coffee/events/313400631/","description":"\#\# What to Expect:

At Beaverton Code and Coffee, we currently have informal hosting with multiple rotating hosts. If you are a regular attendee (2+ times/month), feel free to ask more about it, and I can add you as co-host!

Code and Coffee is an inclusive, informal co-working session. Drink some coffee, bring your laptop, and have engaging conversations. This is a great opportunity to learn from others, share your knowledge, and possibly collaborate on side projects, online courses, or even your day-to-day work tasks.

Still uncertain? Let's chat on Discord! [https://discord.gg/JbGyY3bKTS](https://discord.gg/JbGyY3bKTS)

\#\# Venue:

The Venue is Deadstock Coffee Beaverton, who is graciously allowing us to host a space. WiFi, power outlets. Check them out here: [https://maps.app.goo.gl/gH6zTjoTqssvE5rM9](https://maps.app.goo.gl/gH6zTjoTqssvE5rM9 "https://maps.app.goo.gl/gH6zTjoTqssvE5rM9")

\#\# Agenda:

* **9 AM - Arrival**
* **11:30 AM - Wrapping Up**: You can stay longer, but we'll officially be done!

*After the introduction circle, everything is self-organized. Find a social circle or grab a desk and code.*

\#\# Links:

* Join the Discord: [https://discord.gg/JbGyY3bKTS](https://discord.gg/JbGyY3bKTS)
* Or [https://codeandcoffee.chat/](https://codeandcoffee.chat/)
* If this link is down, visit the main site [https://codeandcoffee.org/](https://codeandcoffee.org/) click on the Discord button!","startDate":"2026-03-09T16:00:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-3-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Deadstock Coffee Beaverton","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Beaverton","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"US","streetAddress":"12305 SW Broadway St, Beaverton, OR 97005,, Beaverton, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Portland Code & Coffee","url":"https://www.meetup.com/portland-code-coffee/"},"performer":"Portland Code & Coffee"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Yoga Integration Workshop+Microdose w/ PNW Integrative Center (separate sign up)","url":"https://www.meetup.com/psychedelic/events/312952928/","description":"This will be the **final** time this class will be before a Microdosing + Mindfulness (Noble Silence) Meditation through the PNW Integrative Center. Microdosing offerings will no longer be offered after March 11th. We’d love to see you at either or both offerings. Sign up for each offering separately.

To learn more and sign up for the 12 pm Microdosing and Mindfulness (Noble Silence) Meditation, go to [pnwintegrativecenter.com.](https://us.app.homecoming.health/p/pnw-integrative-center/microdosing--noble-silence-mon-and-weds-pm) You will first need to complete a [Psilocybin Preparation and Orientation](https://us.app.homecoming.health/p/pnw-integrative-center/psilocybin-preparation-and-orientation) to become a current client. This is an amazing opportunity to be guided through a mini-psychedelic retreat!

**What to expect -**

* \~ 60 minutes of pranayama, asana, and meditation
* wear comfortable clothing you would stretch in
* mats and tools (blocks, straps, etc.) are available
* bring a water bottle
* This [waiver](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScfQLvH9tg83DbOMORCgZTYkk9SxmEpj8AGghU5OO1Nqof1dw/viewform?usp=sf_link) must be signed before attending class.

**About me, Bailey -**

I received my Yoga Teaching Certificate in 2022 with Portland Community College. During my year-long program, I was seeing parallels between the theories of psychedelic integration and the philosophy of yoga. My studies have influenced my mission as an instructor and massage therapist; to hold space for investigation and sensation and to feel as in your body as often as possible.

As a Licensed Massage Therapist (#28882), I like to offer self-care techniques, and lean toward Hatha and Restorative Yoga styles. This is a foundational class, designed to be accessible to any level of yogi.

Bring your intents and lessons with you for reflection and contemplation.

If you would like to book a massage with me, I’m available every other Saturday at the Tiny Massage Cart. Book with me at this [link](https://justbbodywork.my.canva.site/baileycapri), or email me at bailey@tinymassage.com.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**Portland Psychedelic Society** (PPS) is a donation-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.

We ask for a [sliding scale donation of $0-$10](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/donate#DonationFormEmbed) to attend this event. You are not required to pay and no one will be turned away. Cash, Card, PayPal and Venmo are accepted.

PPS Members: your seat is always free!

**[Visit our website](http://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/)** for info about [PPS memberships](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/become-a-member), [volunteer opportunities](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/volunteer), and much more.

Thanks for your participation and support!","startDate":"2026-03-09T17:30:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-5-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"PNW Integrative Center","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"840 SE Washington St, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Portland Psychedelic Society","url":"https://www.meetup.com/psychedelic/"},"performer":"Portland Psychedelic Society"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"🥾2026 - FP - ATC - Hike #09🐇","url":"https://www.meetup.com/edhc_pnw/events/313596225/","description":"**WHEN**: Monday, March 9th, 2026
**TIME**: 10:15am - 3:15pm\*
**WHERE**: UPPER Saltzman Rd./Firelane 7 Trailhead (10820 NW Springville Rd.)
**DISTANCE**: +/- 8.9mi\*
**ELEVATION**: +/- 1,962ft\*
**PACE**: +/- 2.7mph\* Moving Avg.
**DIFFICULTY**: Moderate to Difficult (strenuous uphills)
**DURATION**: +/- 5hrs\*

**🥾Forest Park🌲All Trails Challenge - 2026🐇**

*📜 Holy OutNBackOppotomus! Or something like that…. If you LOVE out and backs, and especially if you don’t, come and join me on this heart pounding tour of Hardesty Trail, Ridge Trail, Gasline Rd/Firelane 7a and Oil Line Rd via Northwest Firelane 7 Road before heading back in relative pieces through the ever wondrous Wildwood to the start.*

*This is hike #09 in a series of 13 hikes that cover the entire 80+ miles of trails in Forest Park. Because not all of the trails can be done as independent loops (and we will be doubling over our path at times) it will require hiking more than 120 miles to complete the entire challenge. If you miss a hike, no worries, we have this series twice a year and makeup hikes can be arranged.*

💪🏽 This is **NOT** a beginner friendly hike, as we will be keeping a decent pace of an average of 2.7mph (or greater!) throughout, could be slightly faster/slower depending on groups’ abilities. I try to go as fast as the slowest person will allow me. These are not good hikes to learn what your pace is.
This can be difficult for some that have sedentary lifestyles, so please don’t be discouraged if at first you don’t succeed, just try try try again!

🚗🚙 Since this is in town, I will not be carpooling myself, but if you wish to do so please organize that on your own in the Event Chat/Comments section.
For those who want to carpool, just know that you should be prepared to drive or take public transit.

🅿️ There are no fees at this location, though space is limited. The address given is for location purposes only.
Trailhead is at the end of UPPER NW Springville Rd. off of NW Skyline Blvd.

**Meetup Coordinates** (Portland): 45.57419° N, 122.78947° W📍
**Trailhead Coordinates** (Portland): 45.57419° N, 122.78947° W📍

**Be Prepared**
• A positive attitude, sense of wonder and wish to be a part of a greater community of truly amazing people❤️
• Weather appropriate clothing/*Adjustable layers* 🧤
• Food, WATER, Snacks and anything you’ll need/want to be comfortable🥪💧
• Our events are cannabis friendly; enjoy responsibly🦉
• Valuables - Better safe than sorry, leave them at home or secure them out of sight in vehicles🚫 = 💍👜📦
• Remember, ALL metrics are estimates only and may be subject to change without notice⚖️
• Community and Respect: Following the event rules is mandatory. Adherence to community guidelines ensures safety and enjoyment for all.

**Expectations**
***\*Safety Comes First!\**** *🚑*
*If you're feeling under the weather, please skip this hike. We prioritize the health and safety of our group!*
***\*The Golden Rule\* 📯***
*No one gets left behind. Hike as a cohesive unit from start to finish*

**A Community Effort🤝**
By RSVP’ing for this event you agree to all Endorphin Drip Hiking Rules and Expectations that are stated in the group bio on the [Main Page], and to all the rules posted for this individual event along with the user agreement [\[Terms of Service\]](https://help.meetup.com/hc/en-us/articles/360027447252-Terms-of-Service?_ga=2.127935439.212460530.1713757034-1761557595.1713627150)
you signed when making an account on Meetup.

*Enjoy these adventures?*

*Join the team and help build the community!*
*[🙇🏻‍♂️Support Endorphin Drip Hiking Club today!](https://blinq.me/oQGwJT0WvtnM?bs=db)*
*Last Four #: 4541*

*🙏🏼Your support helps keep these events free to low charge.*

*Let’s hit the trails and make some memories together!👥*","startDate":"2026-03-09T17:15:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-2-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Upper Saltzman Road Trail Head","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"10520 NW Saltzman Road, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Endorphin Drip Hiking Club","url":"https://www.meetup.com/edhc_pnw/"},"performer":"Endorphin Drip Hiking Club"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Hearts Night - $30 to winner","url":"https://www.meetup.com/meetup-group-umvnakry/events/313629004/","description":"49th Hearts night at McMenamins Barley Mill.

Please expect to play till about 9:00 PM and start at 6:15 PM. If you can't
play till the end, please don't RSVP.

[www.mcmenamins.com](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=9514aab5f0e7c169JmltdHM9MTcwOTA3ODQwMCZpZ3VpZD0wOTBmOWQzOC02MTIzLTYxNDEtMGQzMy04ZDZhNjA4YjYwMWUmaW5zaWQ9NTY1Mg&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=3&fclid=090f9d38-6123-6141-0d33-8d6a608b601e&&ntb=1)

If you do not know how to play, please make sure to download any free Hearts app to your smart phone to familiarize yourself with the game. Newbies are welcome.

We need a minimum 8 players for this event. I'll be adding people from the waitlist in groups of 2. All I ask if that you confirm, that if you can't make it, please give a minimum 1 day advance notice or more, so that we can be assured of getting multiples of 4. If we have an odd number, we will play a game with 3 or 5 players. Again .... cancelling an hour before the event is not a good thing and will yield you a no show without a good excuse.

GAMEPLAY

Each game will have 4 players (or 3 or 5). Beginners are welcome - please indicate in RSVP whether you know how to play Hearts or not. Hearts is an individual game, where you will play against three other players in a game.

Please be cognizant that I will do my utmost to have a multiple of four people show, so please do your utmost to show up if you have RSVP'd, as we are counting on you.

Expectation is that we will start shortly after 6:00 PM and play a minimum of 3 games. Please try to be on time.

We will endeavor to have a small round robin tourney with a minimum 3 games, and the winning player will get a small prize.

NO SHOWS

We have had a couple of instances where participants have decided at the last moment to not show. If you decide to not show, it can affect up to 3 other players. Sometimes circumstances make it impossible to show up, I realize our lives are complicated, but if this happens you need to mark your RSVP. Anyone not showing or cancelling day of without a good excuse will be marked as a no show - I'm very sorry for having to state this.

INTRO TO GAME

See this youtube video if you are new to Hearts and would like an overview of the game. Please watch both for a good intro:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XPWfL-0FGA8

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RSr_89K_65c

===

Meetup leaders are unpaid volunteers and claim no special expertise or knowledge. By attending this meetup, you agree that you are responsible for your own health and safety and decision making and that the organizer(s)/host(s) will not be held liable for any injury or health issue you sustain while in attendance. We will participate as a group but ultimately YOU are responsible for your own experience, safety, and decision-making.

**SCHEDULE**
**6:30 – 7:00PM Silent Meditation**

**7:25PM Closing and Social**
There will be some time after group to connect after practice, after which the group may choose to go out for drinks and snacks at a nearby pub within walking distance.

#onetrust-banner-sdk,
#onetrust-consent-sdk {
  border-radius: 24px !important;
  overflow: hidden;
  box-shadow: 0px 4px 12px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.07) !important; /* X=0, Y=4, Blur=12, 7% opacity */
  border: 1px solid #EBEBEE !important;
  background-color: #FFFFFF !important; /* banner background color */
  background-clip: padding-box;
  color: #69696C !important; /* main text color */
  z-index: 99999 !important; /* ensures it's always on top */
}

/* === TEXT & LINKS === */
#onetrust-banner-sdk p,
#onetrust-banner-sdk span,
#onetrust-banner-sdk div,
#onetrust-consent-sdk p,
#onetrust-consent-sdk span,
#onetrust-consent-sdk div {
  color: #69696C !important; /* main text color */
}

#onetrust-banner-sdk a,
#onetrust-consent-sdk a {
  color: #7661D2 !important; /* link text color */
}

/* === BUTTONS === */
#onetrust-accept-btn-handler,
#onetrust-reject-all-handler,
.ot-sdk-container .ot-sdk-button {
  background-color: #353538 !important; /* Accept & Reject button background */
  color: #FFFFFF !important; /* button text color */
  border-radius: 24px !important;
  border: none !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-btn-handler {
  background-color: #FFFFFF !important; /* Manage Preferences button background */
  color: #353538 !important; /* Manage Preferences text color */
  border: 1px solid #353538 !important; /* Manage Preferences border color */
  border-radius: 24px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-btn-handler:hover {
  background-color: #F7F7F8 !important; /* hover effect for settings button */
}

/* === ACCORDION STYLING === */
.ot-accordion-layout,
.ot-accordion,
.ot-accordion-content {
  background-color: #FFFFFF !important; /* Accordion background color */
}

/* === FOCUS STATES === */
#onetrust-banner-sdk button:focus,
#onetrust-consent-sdk button:focus,
#onetrust-banner-sdk a:focus,
#onetrust-consent-sdk a:focus {
  outline: 2px solid #353538 !important; /* focus border color */
  outline-offset: 2px;
  border-radius: 4px;
}

/* === MOBILE-SPECIFIC STYLING === */
@media (max-width: 768px) {
  #onetrust-banner-sdk,
  #onetrust-consent-sdk {
    right: auto !important;
    width: 90% !important; /* margin on sides */
    overflow-y: auto !important; /* scroll if text is too long */
    z-index: 99999 !important; /* stays above other sticky elements */
  }
}

See","memberPhoto":{"__ref":"PhotoInfo:321744125"}},"Event:313153756":{"__typename":"Event","id":"313153756","dateTime":"2026-03-09T18:30:00-07:00","isAttending":false,"group":{"__typename":"Group","urlname":"psychedelic","__ref":"Group:18210532"},"title":"Psychedelics and Buddhism ","description":"**Our Regular Sessions Include**

* Meditation: We open and close each meeting with a meditation session, creating a mindful atmosphere to engage and reflect.
* Engaging Discussions: Delve into fascinating topics such as the history of psychedelics in Buddhist practices, the concept of ego dissolution, and the parallels between meditative states and psychedelic experiences.
* Deep Dive into the Literature: Explore thought-provoking chapters and imagery from books (like Zig Zag Zen: Buddhism and Psychedelics) and scientific research.
* Sutra Exploration: Compare the profound experiences described in ancient sutras, like The Heart Sutra, with those reported in psychedelic journeys.

**Why You Should Join**

Expand Your Understanding: Gain insights that might inform your psychedelic experiences and possibly your Buddhist practices.

Community and Connection: Share experiences and perspectives with diverse individuals who share your interest in these profound subjects.

Mindful Exploration: Approach the topic with a balance of curiosity and respect, grounded in meditation and mindful discussion.

**Everyone Is Welcome!**

Whether you’re a Zen practitioner, follow another Buddhist tradition, or have no religious affiliation but carry a deep interest in these topics, our meetup is a space for respectful exploration and shared learning.

🌌 Embrace the journey of mind-expanding discussions and spiritual growth. We can't wait to explore the synergies of Psychedelics and Buddhism with you! 🌌

RSVP Today and be part of a unique exploration that bridges ancient wisdom and contemporary psychedelic insights. See you there! 🙏🌺🍄

**About the Moderator**

Zack Prager works on building user experiences that make people happier, healthier, and more mindful at [Mindful Labs](https://www.mindfullabs.com/technology). He studied software development at UCLA and Positive Psychology (science of happiness) at the University of Pennsylvania under Dr. Martin Seligman.

He has a long-standing meditation and yoga practice and writes and produces music for breath work, yoga, and meditation ([Mango Dass](https://mangodass.com/) on Spotify).

His work has been covered by the SF Chronicle, SD Tribune, ABC, CW, NBC, Brit+Co, & more, as well as praised by leading academics.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**Portland Psychedelic Society** (PPS) is a donation-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.

We ask for a [sliding scale donation of $0-$10](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/donate#DonationFormEmbed) to attend this event. You are not required to pay and no one will be turned away. Cash, Card, PayPal and Venmo are accepted.

PPS Members: your seat is always free!

**[Visit our website](http://www.portlandpsychedelic.org)** for info about [PPS memberships](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/become-a-member), [volunteer opportunities](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/volunteer), and much more.

+ Connect Meditation Group","description":"Decompress and connect at the end of your work week with meditation practice and community. This event is in partnership with the Young Meditators Group of Portland.

This open session offers 20 minutes of silent meditation followed by a meditative talking circle practice, and the opportunity to connect and socialize afterwards at a local pub.

**No experience required, and meditators of all traditions are welcome.**
**Cushions will be provided but feel free to bring your own.**

**SCHEDULE**

**6:30 – 7:00PM Silent Meditation**
Silent meditation practice (you are welcome to practice a silent meditation of your choosing)

**7:00 – 7:25PM Meditative Talking Circle**
Resting on the foundation of meditation practice, this talking circle practice expands to include others in the circle. While maintaining a meditation practice, members of the circle are welcome to speak from the present moment, though speaking is not required to participate. Instructions will be provided at the beginning so please arrive on time for this part of the practice.

**7:25PM Closing and Social**
There will be some time after group to connect after practice, after which the group may choose to go out for drinks and snacks at a nearby pub within walking distance.

This is a 'by donation' event. Suggested donation amount $3-10: [Please Donate Here](https://www.paypal.com/donate?hosted_button_id=PPCNJX8KYKY42)

If you are a regular member, please consider signing up for a monthly donation- it helps make this group At Beaverton Code and Coffee, we currently have informal hosting with multiple rotating hosts. If you are a regular attendee (2+ times/month), feel free to ask more about it, and I can add you as co-host!

Code and Coffee is an inclusive, informal co-working session. Drink some coffee, bring your laptop, and have engaging conversations. This is a great opportunity to learn from others, share your knowledge, and possibly collaborate on side projects, online courses, or even your day-to-day work tasks.

Still uncertain? Let's chat on Discord! [https://discord.gg/JbGyY3bKTS](https://discord.gg/JbGyY3bKTS)

\#\# Venue:

The Venue is Deadstock Coffee Beaverton, who is graciously allowing us to host a space. WiFi, power outlets. Check them out here: [https://maps.app.goo.gl/gH6zTjoTqssvE5rM9](https://maps.app.goo.gl/gH6zTjoTqssvE5rM9 "https://maps.app.goo.gl/gH6zTjoTqssvE5rM9")

\#\# Agenda:

* **9 AM - Arrival**
* **11:30 AM - Wrapping Up**: You can stay longer, but we'll officially be done!

*After the introduction circle, everything is self-organized. Find a social circle or grab a desk and code.*

\#\# Links:

* Join the Discord: [https://discord.gg/JbGyY3bKTS](https://discord.gg/JbGyY3bKTS)

Integration Workshop+Microdose w/ PNW Integrative Center (separate sign up)","description":"This will be the **final** time this class will be before a Microdosing + Mindfulness (Noble Silence) Meditation through the PNW Integrative Center. Microdosing offerings will no longer be offered after March 11th. We’d love to see you at either or both offerings. Sign up for each offering separately.

To learn more and sign up for the 12 pm Microdosing and Mindfulness (Noble Silence) Meditation, go to [pnwintegrativecenter.com.](https://us.app.homecoming.health/p/pnw-integrative-center/microdosing--noble-silence-mon-and-weds-pm) You will first need to complete a [Psilocybin Preparation and Orientation](https://us.app.homecoming.health/p/pnw-integrative-center/psilocybin-preparation-and-orientation) to become a current client. This is an amazing opportunity to be guided through a mini-psychedelic retreat!

**What to expect -**

* \~ 60 minutes of pranayama, asana, and meditation
* wear comfortable clothing you would stretch in
* mats and tools (blocks, straps, etc.) are available
* bring a water bottle
* This [waiver](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScfQLvH9tg83DbOMORCgZTYkk9SxmEpj8AGghU5OO1Nqof1dw/viewform?usp=sf_link) must be signed before attending class.

**About me, Bailey -**

I received my Yoga Teaching Certificate in 2022 with Portland Community College. During my year-long program, I was seeing parallels between the theories of psychedelic integration and the philosophy of yoga. My studies have influenced my mission as an instructor and massage therapist; to hold space for investigation and sensation and to feel as in your body as often as possible.

As a Licensed Massage Therapist (#28882), I like to offer self-care techniques, and lean toward Hatha and Restorative Yoga styles. This is a foundational class, designed to be accessible to any level of yogi.

Bring your intents and lessons with you for reflection and contemplation.

If you would like to book a massage with me, I’m available every other Saturday at the Tiny Massage Cart. Book with me at this [link](https://justbbodywork.my.canva.site/baileycapri), or email me at bailey@tinymassage.com.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**Portland Psychedelic Society** (PPS) is a donation-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.

We ask for a [sliding scale donation of $0-$10](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/donate#DonationFormEmbed) to attend this event. You are not required to pay and no one will be turned away. Cash, Card, PayPal and Venmo are accepted.

PPS Members: your seat is always free!

**[Visit our website](http://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/)** for info about [PPS memberships](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/become-a-member), [volunteer opportunities](https://www.portlandpsychedelic.org/volunteer), and much more.

- FP - ATC - Hike #09🐇","dateTime":"2026-03-09T10:15:00-07:00","description":"**WHEN**: Monday, March 9th, 2026
**TIME**: 10:15am - 3:15pm\*
**WHERE**: UPPER Saltzman Rd./Firelane 7 Trailhead (10820 NW Springville Rd.)
**DISTANCE**: +/- 8.9mi\*
**ELEVATION**: +/- 1,962ft\*
**PACE**: +/- 2.7mph\* Moving Avg.
**DIFFICULTY**: Moderate to Difficult (strenuous uphills)
**DURATION**: +/- 5hrs\*

**🥾Forest Park🌲All Trails Challenge - 2026🐇**

*📜 Holy OutNBackOppotomus! Or something like that…. If you LOVE out and backs, and especially if you don’t, come and join me on this heart pounding tour of Hardesty Trail, Ridge Trail, Gasline Rd/Firelane 7a and Oil Line Rd via Northwest Firelane 7 Road before heading back in relative pieces through the ever wondrous Wildwood to the start.*

*This is hike #09 in a series of 13 hikes that cover the entire 80+ miles of trails in Forest Park. Because not all of the trails can be done as independent loops (and we will be doubling over our path at times) it will require hiking more than 120 miles to complete the entire challenge. If you miss a hike, no worries, we have this series twice a year and makeup hikes can be arranged.*

💪🏽 This is **NOT** a beginner friendly hike, as we will be keeping a decent pace of an average of 2.7mph (or greater!) throughout, could be slightly faster/slower depending on groups’ abilities. I try to go as fast as the slowest person will allow me. These are not good hikes to learn what your pace is.
This can be difficult for some that have sedentary lifestyles, so please don’t be discouraged if at first you don’t succeed, just try try try again!

🚗🚙 Since this is in town, I will not be carpooling myself, but if you wish to do so please organize that on your own in the Event Chat/Comments section.
For those who want to carpool, just know that you should be prepared to drive or take public transit.

🅿️ There are no fees at this location, though space is limited. The address given is for location purposes only.
Trailhead is at the end of UPPER NW Springville Rd. off of NW Skyline Blvd.

**Meetup Coordinates** (Portland): 45.57419° N, 122.78947° W📍
**Trailhead Coordinates** (Portland): 45.57419° N, 122.78947° W📍

**Be Prepared**
• A positive attitude, sense of wonder and wish to be a part of a greater community of truly amazing people❤️
• Weather appropriate clothing/*Adjustable layers* 🧤
• Food, WATER, Snacks and anything you’ll need/want to be comfortable🥪💧
• Our events are cannabis friendly; enjoy responsibly🦉
• Valuables - Better safe than sorry, leave them at home or secure them out of sight in vehicles🚫 = 💍👜📦
• Remember, ALL metrics are estimates only and may be subject to change without notice⚖️
• Community and Respect: Following the event rules is mandatory. Adherence to community guidelines ensures safety and enjoyment for all.

**Expectations**
***\*Safety Comes First!\**** *🚑*
*If you're feeling under the weather, please skip this hike. We prioritize the health and safety of our group!*
***\*The Golden Rule\* 📯***
*No one gets left behind. Hike as a cohesive unit from start to finish*

**A Community Effort🤝**

winner","dateTime":"2026-03-09T18:00:00-07:00","description":"49th Hearts night at McMenamins Barley Mill.

Please expect to play till about 9:00 PM and start at 6:15 PM. If you can't
play till the end, please don't RSVP.

[www.mcmenamins.com](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=9514aab5f0e7c169JmltdHM9MTcwOTA3ODQwMCZpZ3VpZD0wOTBmOWQzOC02MTIzLTYxNDEtMGQzMy04ZDZhNjA4YjYwMWUmaW5zaWQ9NTY1Mg&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=3&fclid=090f9d38-6123-6141-0d33-8d6a608b601e&&ntb=1)

If you do not know how to play, please make sure to download any free Hearts app to your smart phone to familiarize yourself with the game. Newbies are welcome.

We need a minimum 8 players for this event. I'll be adding people from the waitlist in groups of 2. All I ask if that you confirm, that if you can't make it, please give a minimum 1 day advance notice or more, so that we can be assured of getting multiples of 4. If we have an odd number, we will play a game with 3 or 5 players. Again .... cancelling an hour before the event is not a good thing and will yield you a no show without a good excuse.

GAMEPLAY

Each game will have 4 players (or 3 or 5). Beginners are welcome - please indicate in RSVP whether you know how to play Hearts or not. Hearts is an individual game, where you will play against three other players in a game.

Please be cognizant that I will do my utmost to have a multiple of four people show, so please do your utmost to show up if you have RSVP'd, as we are counting on you.

Expectation is that we will start shortly after 6:00 PM and play a minimum of 3 games. Please try to be on time.

We will endeavor to have a small round robin tourney with a minimum 3 games, and the winning player will get a small prize.

NO SHOWS

We have had a couple of instances where participants have decided at the last moment to not show. If you decide to not show, it can affect up to 3 other players. Sometimes circumstances make it impossible to show up, I realize our lives are complicated, but if this happens you need to mark your RSVP. Anyone not showing or cancelling day of without a good excuse will be marked as a no show - I'm very sorry for having to state this.

INTRO TO GAME

See this youtube video if you are new to Hearts and would like an overview of the game. Please watch both for a good intro:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XPWfL-0FGA8

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RSr_89K_65c

Monday + Friday Night Meditation Practice","description":"Decompress and connect at the end of your work week with meditation practice and community. This event is in partnership with the Young Meditators Group of Portland.

This open session offers 20 minutes of silent meditation followed by a meditative talking circle practice, and the opportunity to connect and socialize afterwards at a local pub.

**No experience required, and meditators of all traditions are welcome.**
**Cushions will be provided but feel free to bring your own.**

**SCHEDULE**
**6:30 – 7:00PM Silent Meditation**
Silent meditation practice (you are welcome to practice a silent meditation of your choosing)

**7:00 – 7:25PM Meditative Talking Circle**
Resting on the foundation of meditation practice, this talking circle practice expands to include others in the circle. While maintaining a meditation practice, members of the circle are welcome to speak from the present moment, though speaking is not required to participate. Instructions will be provided at the beginning so please arrive on time for this part of the practice.

**7:25PM Closing and Social**
There will be some time after group to connect after practice, after which the group may choose to go out for drinks and snacks at a nearby pub within walking distance.

This is a 'by donation' event. Suggested donation amount $3-10: [Please Donate Here](https://www.paypal.com/donate?hosted_button_id=PPCNJX8KYKY42)
If you are a regular member, please consider signing up for a monthly donation- it helps make this group
